diagrams.title: Arithmetic Sequence Visualization
diagrams.description: A horizontal number line showing an arithmetic sequence where each term increases by a constant difference d. Terms are represented as points with labeled values a₁, a₂, a₃, a₄, and the constant difference d is explicitly shown between consecutive terms.
diagrams.purpose: Helps students visualize how an arithmetic sequence progresses linearly with a constant difference between consecutive terms, reinforcing the definition aₙ = a₁ + (n-1)d

diagrams.title: Geometric Sequence Visualization
diagrams.description: A set of vertical bars or blocks of increasing/decreasing heights representing a geometric sequence with constant ratio r. Each term a₁, a₂, a₃, a₄ is shown with arrows indicating multiplication by r to get the next term, demonstrating exponential growth or decay.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the concept of geometric sequences where each term is obtained by multiplying the previous term by a constant ratio, showing exponential change rather than linear

diagrams.title: Summation as Area Accumulation
diagrams.description: A staircase-like diagram showing partial sums of a series. Each term aᵢ is represented as a rectangle of width 1 and height aᵢ. The cumulative height at each position represents the partial sum Sₙ = Σᵢ₌₁ⁿ aᵢ, with labels showing the sigma notation and corresponding numerical values.
elements: partial sum labels (S₁, S₂, S₃, Sₙ)
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how summations work as accumulating area under discrete points, helping students understand the connection between the abstract sigma notation and the concrete concept of adding up sequential terms to form partial sums
